steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Use the volume formula to find height
steps.content:
The volume of a rectangular prism is $V = \text{length} \times \text{width} \times \text{height}$.

Substitute the given values: $$2,736 = 19 \times 8 \times h = 152h$$
Divide both sides by 152: $$h = \frac{2,736}{152} = 18$$
This matches the correct answer — A is correct.
